commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human maintainer/security review is needed because the blocker is the sandbox precedence contract and contributor live proof, not a safe automated repair marker.; Needs attention: The diff changes sandbox mount-boundary behavior and can let configured binds shadow protected read-only skill roots.; Review finding: [P1] Keep protected skill mounts authoritative — src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.ts:142 - [P2... (sha=111a164176079885389746da3b580521ea1ab9aa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR filters read-only workspace skill mounts out of shell, browser, and filesystem sandbox mount modeling when configured Docker binds target the same container path, with focused tests and a demo script.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +71, Other +85. Total +199 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source-level reproduction is high confidence because current main and v2026.6.10 append custom binds before protected read-only skill mounts in shell and browser Docker create args when the dangerous reserved-target override allows the path; I did not run a live Docker daemon in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer/security review is needed because the blocker is the sandbox precedence contract and contributor live proof, not a safe automated repair marker.

target.title: fix(sandbox): skip read-only skill mounts when user binds target the same container path
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw.json` `sandbox.docker.binds` entries targeting reserved container paths (e.g. `/workspace/skills`, `/workspace/.agents/skills`) cause the Docker daemon to reject container creation with `Duplicate mount point`. The internal `resolveReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMounts` unconditionally appends read-only skill mounts without checking whether a user-defined bind already targets the same container path.
- Why now: Any user with custom docker binds that happen to overlap a skill mount path cannot launch sandbox containers at all. The gateway fails before the agent can start.
- What changed:
  - New `filterReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountsByBinds()` in `workspace-mounts.ts` parses user bind specs and excludes skill mounts whose container path is already targeted
  - `ensureSandboxContainer` in `docker.ts` applies the filter before config hash computation and container creation
  - `ensureSandboxBrowser` in `browser.ts` applies the filter before browser container creation
  - `buildSandboxFsMounts` in `fs-paths.ts` applies the filter to keep the FS path resolution table consistent
  - Normalizes trailing slashes in container paths during comparison (e.g., `/workspace/skills/` matches `/workspace/skills`)
- What did NOT change: Guard mounts in `agent-tools.ts` are unchanged — they don't generate Docker `-v` args. The `appendWorkspaceMountArgs` fallback path is untouched because production callers always pass `includeReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMounts: false`. No new dependencies added.
- Outcome: User binds that conflict with skill mount paths no longer cause `Duplicate mount point`. User binds take precedence. Config hashes are computed from the effective mount list.
- Reviewer focus: `workspace-mounts.ts` `filterReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountsByBinds` function, and the three integration points in `docker.ts`, `browser.ts`, `fs-paths.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #93854

Was this requested by a maintainer?
- No, but the issue has clear reproduction steps and root cause analysis.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `resolveReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMounts` unconditionally appends internal read-only skill mounts for `rw` workspaces. When a user bind targets the same container path, the system adds a second `-v` spec for the same Docker target, and the daemon rejects the container with `Duplicate mount point`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No pre-existing check for container-path overlap between user binds and internal skill mounts. Previous tests assumed Docker would accept duplicate targets and "last one wins", but Docker 29.x rejects duplicate mount points outright.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw.json` `sandbox.docker.binds` with `/host/custom-skills:/workspace/skills:rw` must not produce a second `-v path:/workspace/skills:ro,z` — that second mount would cause Docker to reject the container with `Duplicate mount point`.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10 x64, Node 24.17.0, pnpm 11.2.2, branch `fix/sandbox-bind-conflict`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  node scripts/demo-sandbox-bind-filter.mjs
  pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ node scripts/demo-sandbox-bind-filter.mjs
  Workspace: C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-6HohAN

  Resolved skill mounts (no binds):
    C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-6HohAN\skills -> /workspace/skills

  User binds: [ '/host/custom-skills:/workspace/skills:rw' ]

  Filtered skill mounts (after skipping conflicts):
    (none — conflicting mount was filtered out)

  Docker -v args that WOULD be generated (one per mount):
    -v C:\...\skills:/workspace/skills:ro,z

  Docker -v args that ARE generated (after filtering):

  Conflicting mount at /workspace/skills in filtered output: false
  ✅ PASS: Conflicting skill mount filtered out — no Duplicate mount point from Docker

  $ pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/
   Test Files  22 passed (22)
        Tests  224 passed (224)
     Start at  00:27:03
     Duration  9.90s
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The conflicting `/workspace/skills` skill mount is absent from the filtered output. Docker receives only the user's bind, avoiding the `Duplicate mount point` error. All 224 sandbox tests pass.
- What was not tested: Live end-to-end Docker container creation against a real Docker 29.x daemon. The proof shows the function correctly filters the mount list that would be passed to `docker create`.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Sandbox environment without a Docker daemon — the proof exercises the exact `-v` argument list that would be sent to Docker.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `node scripts/demo-sandbox-bind-filter.mjs`
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/`
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/browser.create.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- Added 8 new unit tests for `filterReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountsByBinds` in `workspace-mounts.test.ts`:
  1. `returns all mounts when binds is undefined`
  2. `returns all mounts when binds is empty`
  3. `filters out a mount whose container path matches a user bind`
  4. `filters out multiple mounts when multiple binds conflict`
  5. `returns all mounts when no bind targets conflict`
  6. `filters out all mounts when every container path is targeted by a bind`
  7. `handles rw binds (no ro option) correctly`
  8. `normalizes container paths before comparing`
- Updated 2 existing integration tests:
  1. `docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts`: asserts conflicting mount is absent instead of appended last
  2. `browser.create.test.ts`: same update for the browser sandbox path
- Added 1 live proof script: `scripts/demo-sandbox-bind-filter.mjs`
target.author: chenyangjun-xy

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
